meta.title: Does Eastern Orthodoxy Have the "Fullness of the Faith?"
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: What two models does Ortlund use to describe how the 'one true church' claim has been construed?
questions.answer: Hierarchical and Noah's Ark

questions.question: According to Ortlund, which model of the 'one true church' claim represents the historic Eastern Orthodox position from the 9th to 19th centuries?
questions.answer: The Noah's Ark view

questions.question: Who described discovering in Orthodoxy 'the fullness of the faith' in an interview that prompted this video?
questions.answer: Jonathan Pageau

questions.question: What is the filioque?
options: The doctrine that the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father and the Son
The doctrine that the pope has universal jurisdiction
The Eastern Orthodox doctrine of theosis
The claim that icons should be venerated
questions.answer: The doctrine that the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father and the Son

questions.question: Paisius Velichkovsky described the filioque as what?
options: The first and most important of all the heresies
questions.answer: The first and most important of all the heresies

questions.question: According to Velichkovsky, what must uniates do to have hope of salvation?
options: Pray to the saints for intercession
Openly renounce the filioque and reunite with the Orthodox Church
Accept the authority of the Ecumenical Patriarch
questions.answer: Openly renounce the filioque and reunite with the Orthodox Church

questions.question: What are Uniate churches?
options: Protestant churches that use Eastern liturgy
Eastern churches in communion with Rome that retain their own liturgy
Orthodox churches that reject the Ecumenical Patriarch
Churches that formed after the 1054 schism
questions.answer: Eastern churches in communion with Rome that retain their own liturgy

questions.question: What prompted the 1672 Synod of Jerusalem to produce the Confession of Dositheus?
options: Pope Leo XIII's invitation to unity
questions.answer: Cyril Lucaris expressing Protestant-leaning views

questions.question: According to the Confession of Dositheus, what happens when people forsake the church?
options: They are forsaken by the Holy Spirit and only darkness and blindness remain
They can still be saved through good works
They are placed in a lower tier of participation
questions.answer: They are forsaken by the Holy Spirit and only darkness and blindness remain

questions.question: The Patriarchal Encyclical of 1895 was issued in response to whom?
options: Pius IX's epistle to the Eastern churches
Leo XIII's invitation to unity with Rome
The Council of Florence's Cantate Domino
questions.answer: Leo XIII's invitation to unity with Rome

questions.question: The 1848 Encyclical of the Eastern Patriarchs placed the filioque on the same level of heresy as what?
questions.answer: Arianism

questions.question: How many Eastern Patriarchs issued the 1848 Encyclical?
questions.answer: Four

questions.question: Which 19th-century Metropolitan of Moscow used Noah's Ark imagery in his Longer Catechism?
questions.answer: St. Philaret

questions.question: What is the logical chain by which the filioque leads to damnation in historic Orthodox thought?
options: Filioque = bad theology → bad theology = imperfect worship → imperfect worship = reduced grace
Filioque = heresy → heresy = outside the church → outside the church = no sacramental grace → no grace = no salvation
Filioque = schism → schism = excommunication → excommunication = purgatory
Filioque = disobedience → disobedience = loss of merit → loss of merit = damnation
questions.answer: Filioque = heresy → heresy = outside the church → outside the church = no sacramental grace → no grace = no salvation

questions.question: What does Ortlund argue is distinctive about the Protestant position on the 'one true church'?
options: Protestants deny the existence of a true church
Protestants reject institutional exclusivism while retaining doctrinal boundaries
Protestants believe every institution is equally the true church
Protestants believe the true church ceased to exist after the apostles
questions.answer: Protestants reject institutional exclusivism while retaining doctrinal boundaries

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what did Luther and Calvin recognize about churches outside Protestantism?
options: They were true churches bearing the Holy Spirit's work
They needed to adopt Protestant liturgy to be valid
questions.answer: They were true churches bearing the Holy Spirit's work

questions.question: When Lutherans wrote to Ecumenical Patriarch Jeremiah II in the 1570s, how did they address him?
questions.answer: As a fellow Christian

questions.question: What is the visible/invisible church distinction in Protestant theology?
options: The visible church is real and the invisible church is metaphorical
The visible church is all baptized Christians; the invisible church is those truly united to Christ, known only to God
The visible church is the building and the invisible church is the congregation
The visible church is the clergy and the invisible church is the laity
questions.answer: The visible church is all baptized Christians; the invisible church is those truly united to Christ, known only to God

questions.question: Who does Ortlund cite as an example of someone who was in the visible church but not the invisible church?
questions.answer: Judas Iscariot

questions.question: What counterargument did Dave Armstrong make that Ortlund addresses?
options: That the Orthodox Church has valid sacraments outside Roman Catholicism
That since valid baptism occurs outside the Roman Catholic Church, salvation must occur outside it
That Protestants have no apostolic succession
That the filioque is a minor theological disagreement
questions.answer: That since valid baptism occurs outside the Roman Catholic Church, salvation must occur outside it

questions.question: How does the Cantate Domino respond to the idea that valid sacraments outside the church confer salvation?
options: It affirms that baptism always saves regardless of institutional membership
It says sacraments contribute to salvation only for those who abide in the ecclesiastical body
It declares all non-Catholic baptisms invalid
It acknowledges salvation through any valid sacrament
questions.answer: It says sacraments contribute to salvation only for those who abide in the ecclesiastical body

questions.question: What does Catholic scholar Francis Sullivan document about the medieval tradition?
options: That implicit faith was always accepted as sufficient
That explicit Christian faith was required for the salvation of everyone in the Christian era
That the medieval church was tolerant of doctrinal diversity
That the filioque was accepted by Eastern theologians
questions.answer: That explicit Christian faith was required for the salvation of everyone in the Christian era

questions.question: Ortlund argues that modern reinterpretations involving 'invisible membership' or 'implicit submission' are what?
options: Faithful extensions of the medieval tradition
Anachronistic innovations with no pre-modern precedent
questions.answer: Anachronistic innovations with no pre-modern precedent

questions.question: What practical concern does Ortlund raise about the infallible magisterium?
options: It is too small to interpret all of Scripture
If everyone can misinterpret the magisterium for 600 years, its practical utility is greatly reduced
It has never issued a binding doctrinal statement
It agrees with the Orthodox position on the filioque
questions.answer: If everyone can misinterpret the magisterium for 600 years, its practical utility is greatly reduced

questions.question: Which early church father does Ortlund mention as having spoken of pagans who lived justly as members of the body of Christ?
questions.answer: Justin Martyr

questions.question: What pastoral concern does Ortlund identify as the core problem with exclusivist ecclesiology?
options: It produces ecclesial anxiety and can intimidate people into conversion through fear
questions.answer: It produces ecclesial anxiety and can intimidate people into conversion through fear

questions.question: What verse does Ortlund cite to assure believers that their salvation is secure?
options: John 5:24 — 'Whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life'
questions.answer: John 5:24 — 'Whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life'

questions.question: According to Ortlund, when did a sizable alternative to exclusivism first appear in Eastern Orthodoxy?
questions.answer: The 20th century

questions.question: Which document does Ortlund cite as declaring that submission to the Roman Pontiff is necessary for salvation?
questions.answer: Unam Sanctam

questions.question: What period does Ortlund focus his historical survey on?
questions.answer: The 9th to 19th centuries
